---
title: "Endangered Languages of the World"
subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2026 - Final Project"
author: "Damon Kelson"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: images/watercolour_sys02_img34_teacup-ocean.jpg
  data-background-size: stretch
  data-background-opacity: "0.7"
  data-slide-number: none
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:  ['data/customtheming.scss']
  
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| include: false

# Load packages here
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, kableExtra, forcats, sf, ggrepel,
               patchwork, cowplot, rnaturalearth, rnaturalearthdata, hexbin)

```

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Plot theme
#ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11))

# For better figure resolution
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(
#  fig.retina = 3, 
#  dpi = 300, 
#  fig.width = 6, 
#  fig.asp = 0.618 
#  )
```

# Presentation planning

```{r Initial csv assignment and prep}
languages <- read_csv("data/languages.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
status    <- read_csv("data/endangered_status.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
families  <- read_csv("data/families.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

status_order <- status |>
  distinct(status_code, status_label) |>
  arrange(status_code) |>
  pull(status_label)
```

```{r Data prep}
unclear_groups <- c("Bookkeeping", "Unclassifiable", "Pidgin",
                     "Speech Register", "Sign Language", "Unattested",
                     "Artificial Language", "Mixed Language")
# ~10% of the data set!

family_size <- languages |>
  filter(!is.na(family_id)) |>
  count(family_id, name = "family_size")
```

```{r Base data}
lang_base <- languages |>
  left_join(status, by = "id") |>
  left_join(families, by = c("family_id" = "id")) |>
  left_join(family_size, by = "family_id") |>
  mutate(
    status_label = factor(status_label, levels = status_order, ordered = TRUE),
    # 3-category split: 1 | 2,3,4,5 | 6... NA kept around (~5%)
    risk_group = case_when(
      status_code == 1     ~ "not endangered",
      status_code %in% 2:5 ~ "at risk",
      status_code == 6     ~ "extinct"
    ),
    risk_group = factor(risk_group, levels = c("not endangered", "at risk", "extinct")),
    is_unclear_group = family %in% unclear_groups,
    family_bucket = case_when(
      is_isolate         ~ "isolate",
      is_unclear_group    ~ "unclear (exclude)",
      family_size <= 10   ~ "small (2-10)",
      family_size <= 50   ~ "medium (11-50)",
      family_size > 50    ~ "large (50+)"
    ),
    family_bucket = factor(family_bucket, levels = c(
      "isolate", "small (2-10)", "medium (11-50)", "large (50+)",
      "unclear (exclude)"
    ))
  )
```

```{r Map prep}
lang_geo <- lang_base |> filter(!is.na(latitude), !is.na(longitude))

world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

hex_bins <- 35    # constant tweaks

# map with extinction dropped
map_data_excl_extinct <- lang_geo |>
  filter(risk_group %in% c("not endangered", "at risk")) |>
  mutate(at_risk_num = as.numeric(risk_group == "at risk"))

family_severity <- lang_base |>
  filter(!is.na(risk_group), family_bucket != "unclear (exclude)") |>
  count(family_bucket, risk_group) |>
  group_by(family_bucket) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
```

```{r plot exploration 3}
diverging_data <- family_severity |>
  filter(risk_group %in% c("not endangered", "extinct")) |>
  mutate(
    pct_signed = if_else(risk_group == "extinct", -pct, pct),
    label_hjust = if_else(risk_group == "extinct", 1.1, -0.1)
  )

ggplot(diverging_data, aes(family_bucket, pct_signed, fill = risk_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(abs(pct_signed), accuracy = 1), hjust = label_hjust)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("not endangered" = "blue", "extinct" = "red")) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Not-endangered and extinct",
       x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
```

```{r plot exploration 4}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, fill = "grey95") +
  stat_summary_hex(data = map_data_excl_extinct, aes(longitude, latitude, z = at_risk_num),
                   fun = mean, bins = hex_bins, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = c("#0077B6", "#00B4D8", "#FDE725", "#E63946")) +
  coord_sf() +
  labs(title = "Share at risk (extinct excluded)", fill = "% at risk") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r hex refinement prep}
# ECK4 equal-area pseudocyrlindrical projection
eck4 <- "+proj=eck4"

lang_sf    <- st_as_sf(map_data_excl_extinct, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326) |>
  st_transform(eck4)
world_proj <- st_transform(world, eck4)
```

```{r hex prep}
grid <- st_make_grid(world_proj, cellsize = 500000, square = FALSE) |>
  st_as_sf() |>
  mutate(cell_id = row_number())

cell_summary <- st_join(lang_sf, grid) |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  group_by(cell_id) |>
  summarize(pct_at_risk = mean(at_risk_num), n_lang = n(), .groups = "drop")

grid_plot <- grid |>
  left_join(cell_summary, by = "cell_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(n_lang)) |>
  mutate(alpha_val = scales::rescale(sqrt(n_lang), to = c(0.45, 1)))
```

```{r hex plot}
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_proj, fill = "grey97", color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.15) +
  geom_sf(data = grid_plot, aes(fill = pct_at_risk, alpha = alpha_val), color = NA) +
  geom_sf(data = world_proj, fill = NA, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_alpha_identity() +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "OrRd", direction = 1) +
  coord_sf(crs = eck4) +
  labs(title = "Share at risk (extinct excluded)", fill = "% at risk")
```

```{r isolates diverging prep}
p_bar <- ggplot(diverging_data, aes(family_bucket, pct_signed, fill = risk_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(abs(pct_signed), accuracy = 1), hjust = label_hjust)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("not endangered" = "blue", "extinct" = "red")) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
```

```{r iso prep 2}
iso_geo <- lang_base |> filter(is_isolate, !is.na(latitude), !is.na(longitude))
iso_sf  <- st_as_sf(iso_geo, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)

sa_box <- list(lon = c(-85, -34), lat = c(-20, 13))
oc_box <- list(lon = c(110, 175), lat = c(-45, 5))
```

```{r iso regions}
iso_region <- iso_sf |>
  mutate(
    region_focus = case_when(
      between(latitude, sa_box$lat[1], sa_box$lat[2]) & between(longitude, sa_box$lon[1], sa_box$lon[2]) ~ "Northern South America",
      between(latitude, oc_box$lat[1], oc_box$lat[2]) & between(longitude, oc_box$lon[1], oc_box$lon[2]) ~ "Australia, New Guinea & Oceania",
      TRUE ~ "elsewhere"
    )
  ) |>
  st_drop_geometry()
```

```{r iso plots}
p_map_base <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, fill = "grey95", color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_point(data = iso_geo, aes(longitude, latitude, color = risk_group), size = 1.6) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_sa <- p_map_base + coord_sf(xlim = sa_box$lon, ylim = sa_box$lat, expand = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Northern South America")
p_oc <- p_map_base + coord_sf(xlim = oc_box$lon, ylim = oc_box$lat, expand = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Australia, New Guinea & Oceania")
```

```{r iso callout}
focus_pct <- mean(iso_region$region_focus != "elsewhere")

p_callout <- ggplot() +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0.65, label = scales::percent(focus_pct, accuracy = 1),
           size = 9, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0.25, size = 3.2,
           label = "of all isolates worldwide sit in northern South America\nor Australia, New Guinea & Oceania") +
  xlim(-1, 1) + ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2))
```

```{r iso combine}
p_callout / (p_bar | (p_sa / p_oc)) + plot_layout(heights = c(0.25, 0.75))
```



```{r plot exploration 5}
#lang_base |> 
#  dplyr::filter(is_unclear_group == TRUE) |> 
#  View()
```




# Using Quarto for presentations

## Quarto

- The presentation is created using the Quarto CLI

- `##` sets the start of a new slide

## Layouts

You can use plain text

::::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
- or bullet points[^1]
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
or in two columns
:::
:::::

[^1]: And add footnotes

- like

- this

## Plot and text

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
- Some text

- goes here
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, warning=FALSE, fig.width=5.5}
#penguins |>
#  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = species, color = species)) +
#  geom_boxplot(linewidth = 0.75,
#               outlier.size = 2.5) +
#  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
#  theme(legend.key.size = unit(0.8, "cm"))
```
:::
:::::

# A new section...

## Tables

If you want to generate a table, make sure it is in the HTML format (instead of Markdown or other formats), e.g.,

```{r}
#penguins |> 
#  head() |>
#  kableExtra::kable(format = "html") |>
#  kableExtra::kable_styling()
```

## Feeling adventurous?

- You are welcomed to use the default styling of the slides. In fact, that's what I expect majority of you will do. You will differentiate yourself with the content of your presentation.

- But some of you might want to play around with slide styling. Some solutions for this can be found at https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs.
